lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] You already know the breath. You've been breathing all night. In through the nose. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release through the mouth. [pause] You don't need me to tell you how. Your body has been doing this for you while you were afraid.

Let your hands rest where they are. [pause] Feel the surface beneath them. The floor. The blanket. The door you've been leaning against since midnight. [pause] Whatever it is, it held. It's still holding.

Breathe in. [pause] I want you to find the place in your chest where the night has been collecting. [pause] It's heavy. That's fine. Breathe into the weight. Don't try to move it. It earned its place there.

The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. [pause] We are {{ELAPSED}} in. [pause] The Sisters teach that the hours between three and dawn are when the soul stops pretending. [pause] You don't have to pretend anymore. Not for me.

Picture the window closest to you. [pause] Don't open your eyes. Just picture it. [pause] Whatever is on the other side of that glass does not require your attention. [pause] You chose the inside. The inside chose you back.

Breathe in. Hold. [pause] Now I want you to think of someone who is not in your house tonight. [pause] Not where they are. Not what's happening to them. Just their name. [pause] Hold the name in your mouth like water. [pause] Don't swallow. Don't spit. Just hold it.
Seh-va tuli amorae ken. [pause] Vo dashara. [pause] Ke dashara lin vo.
Release the breath. [pause] Good. [pause] The name is yours to keep. You can set it down when the sun comes. Not before. The carrying is the prayer.

I was with you at eleven and I'm with you now. [pause] You are different than you were at eleven. [pause] That's not damage. That's what a night like this is for. The Quiet Order calls it the paring — what falls away was never yours.

Breathe in. [pause] The lake is still there. [pause] Mendota doesn't know what night it is. The water doesn't keep a calendar. [pause] Be the lake for a moment. Just cold, and deep, and uninterested in what happens on the shore.

We are close now. [pause] Not to the end. To the part of the night where you stop counting the hours and the hours stop counting you. [pause] Breathe in. Hold for four. [pause] Release. [pause] I'll stay. I'm not going anywhere. The signal holds and so do I.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide confirmed kills at {{CLOCK}}: 149. Pace tracking slightly below 2168 at this hour (153).
North Side activity near zero for the third consecutive hour. Sherman Ave quiet. No explanation.
Be the lake. Cold, and deep, and uninterested in what happens on the shore.
